questions.question: How many players are in a basketball team on court?
questions.explanation: Each team has 5 players on the basketball court.

questions.question: Which country won the FIFA World Cup 2022?
questions.explanation: Argentina won the 2022 FIFA World Cup.

questions.question: What is the length of a tennis court?
questions.explanation: A tennis court is 23.77 meters long.

questions.question: How many innings are there in cricket?
questions.explanation: There are 2 innings in a cricket match.

questions.question: What is the highest score in darts?
questions.explanation: The highest score in darts is 180 (three triple 20s).

questions.question: What is the national sport of Japan?
questions.explanation: Sumo Wrestling is Japan's national sport.

questions.question: How many rings are there in the Olympic flag?
questions.explanation: The Olympic flag has 5 interlocking rings.

questions.question: In which sport would you perform a 'slam dunk'?
questions.explanation: A slam dunk is a basketball move.

questions.question: How often are the Summer Olympic Games held?
questions.explanation: The Summer Olympics are held every 4 years.

questions.question: Which country has won the most FIFA World Cups?
questions.explanation: Brazil has won the FIFA World Cup 5 times.

questions.question: What is the chemical symbol for Gold?
questions.explanation: Au is the chemical symbol for Gold.

questions.question: What is the smallest unit of life?
questions.explanation: Cell is the smallest unit of life.

questions.question: What is the speed of light?
questions.explanation: The speed of light is approximately 300,000 km/s.

questions.question: What is the most abundant element in the universe?
questions.explanation: Hydrogen is the most abundant element in the universe.

questions.question: What is the boiling point of water at sea level?
questions.explanation: Water boils at 100°C at sea level.

questions.question: What planet is known as the Red Planet?
questions.explanation: Mars appears red due to iron oxide on its surface.

questions.question: What gas do humans need to breathe to survive?
questions.explanation: Humans need oxygen for respiration.

questions.question: What is the powerhouse of the cell?
questions.explanation: Mitochondria produce energy for the cell.

questions.question: What force keeps us on the ground?
questions.explanation: Gravity pulls objects toward Earth's center.

questions.question: How many bones are in the adult human body?
questions.explanation: An adult human body has 206 bones.

questions.question: Who is the founder of Microsoft?
questions.explanation: Bill Gates founded Microsoft.

questions.question: What does HTML stand for?
questions.explanation: HTML stands for Hyper Text Markup Language.

questions.question: In which year was the first iPhone released?
questions.explanation: The first iPhone was released in 2007.

questions.question: What does CPU stand for?
questions.explanation: CPU stands for Central Processing Unit.

questions.question: Which programming language is known as the 'language of the web'?
questions.explanation: JavaScript is known as the language of the web.

questions.question: Who is considered the founder of the World Wide Web?
questions.explanation: Tim Berners-Lee invented the World Wide Web in 1989.

questions.question: What does 'AI' stand for?
questions.explanation: AI stands for Artificial Intelligence.

questions.question: Which company developed the Android OS?
questions.explanation: Android was developed by Google.

questions.question: What does 'RAM' stand for?
questions.explanation: RAM stands for Random Access Memory.

questions.question: Which company created the Flutter framework?
questions.explanation: Flutter was developed by Google.

questions.question: In which year did World War II end?
questions.explanation: World War II ended in 1945.

questions.question: Who was the first President of the United States?
questions.explanation: George Washington was the first President of the United States.

questions.question: In which year did the Titanic sink?
questions.explanation: The Titanic sank in 1912.

questions.question: Who invented the printing press?
questions.explanation: Johannes Gutenberg invented the printing press.

questions.question: In which year did the Berlin Wall fall?
questions.explanation: The Berlin Wall fell in 1989.

questions.question: Who invented the telephone?
questions.explanation: Alexander Graham Bell invented the telephone in 1876.

questions.question: Which civilization built the pyramids of Giza?
questions.explanation: The pyramids of Giza were built by ancient Egyptians.

questions.question: In which year did India gain independence?
questions.explanation: India gained independence from British rule in 1947.

questions.question: Who was known as the 'Iron Lady'?
questions.explanation: Margaret Thatcher was called the 'Iron Lady'.

questions.question: Which ancient wonder was located in Egypt?
questions.explanation: The Great Pyramid of Giza is the only ancient wonder still standing.

questions.question: What is the capital of France?
questions.explanation: Paris is the capital of France.

questions.question: What is the largest ocean in the world?
questions.explanation: The Pacific Ocean is the largest ocean in the world.

questions.question: How many continents are there?
questions.explanation: There are 7 continents in the world.

questions.question: What is the currency of Japan?
questions.explanation: The Yen is the currency of Japan.

questions.question: Which country is home to the kangaroo?
questions.explanation: Australia is home to the kangaroo.

questions.question: What is the largest planet in our solar system?
questions.explanation: Jupiter is the largest planet in our solar system.

questions.question: What is the smallest country in the world?
questions.explanation: Vatican City is the smallest country by area.

questions.question: Which language has the most native speakers worldwide?
questions.explanation: Mandarin Chinese has the most native speakers.

questions.question: How many colors are there in a rainbow?
questions.explanation: A rainbow has 7 colors: VIBGYOR.

questions.question: Which is the longest river in the world?
questions.explanation: The Nile River is generally considered the longest river.
